sheet_gelatin.name: Titanium
sheet_gelatin.notes: Softest common sheet grade. Industry standard.

sheet_gelatin.name: Silver
sheet_gelatin.notes: Industry-standard sheet grade. Common in home baking.

sheet_gelatin.name: Gold
sheet_gelatin.notes: Industry-standard sheet grade. Most common in professional pastry recipes.

sheet_gelatin.name: Platinum
sheet_gelatin.notes: Firmest common sheet grade. Industry standard.

powder_gelatin.name: Knox Unflavored
powder_gelatin.notes: Most common US grocery powder. Bloom strength is not printed on retail packaging but has been independently confirmed.

powder_gelatin.name: Generic US grocery powder (brand unlisted)
powder_gelatin.notes: Most US grocery unflavored gelatin powders cluster in this range. When the brand does not publish a bloom value, 215 is a reasonable estimate.

powder_gelatin.name: European pastry-grade powder
powder_gelatin.notes: Typical for European pastry applications. Check packaging for exact value when possible.

powder_gelatin.name: I don't know / not labeled
powder_gelatin.notes: If you can't identify your gelatin, 215 is a reasonable middle-of-the-road assumption. Results may be slightly off — if your final gel is too firm or too loose, adjust by 10-15% next time.
